task_details.delay_risks: Seasonal demand fluctuations may lead to underutilization or overcapacity in programming, especially if winter programs fail to attract sufficient participation. Delays in securing partnerships with hotel providers could delay the rollout of tourist packages.
task_details.mitigation_strategy: Conduct pre-season demand forecasting using historical data and community surveys; pilot-test themed winter programs with small groups before full launch. Establish early contracts with at least three hotel partners to secure distribution channels for tourist packages.

task_details.delay_risks: Winter weather conditions in Nuuk may limit public attendance, and local interest in indoor cultural activities might be lower than expected during peak winter months. Scheduling conflicts with other community events could reduce turnout.
task_details.mitigation_strategy: Design 'Winter Pottery Nights' with flexible timing (evening and weekend slots) and promote through targeted outreach via schools, elders’ council, and social media. Offer incentives such as free materials or raffle prizes to boost initial participation.

task_details.delay_risks: Summer tourism peaks may overwhelm staff capacity if micro-courses are not scaled appropriately. Tourist demand may be unpredictable due to fluctuating travel patterns or external factors like flight disruptions.
task_details.mitigation_strategy: Develop modular, short-duration micro-courses (1–2 hours) that can be delivered by rotating instructors. Partner with hotels to co-promote packages and implement a real-time booking system with automated capacity alerts to prevent overbooking.
